items.context: Beck's humorous term for the curved, winding lines of the previous geographically-accurate Underground map that he replaced with straight lines and geometric angles.
items.publicReaction: The characterization demonstrates Beck's wit and clear aesthetic judgment regarding the inadequacies of the existing design.
items.source: Multiple sources including City Monitor and biographical accounts

items.text: This diagram is an evolution of the original design conceived in 1931 by Harry Beck.
items.context: Official attribution statement that began appearing on every London Underground map from 1997 onwards, 23 years after Beck's death, formally acknowledging his fundamental contribution.
items.publicReaction: This posthumous recognition by London Transport provided long-overdue official acknowledgment of Beck's pioneering work, though it came decades after his struggles with the organization.
items.source: London Underground official maps; English Heritage Blue Plaque material
